reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into Spanish, preserving code blocks, MDX components, frontmatter, headings, etc. Also need to adjust relative URLs to have ../ prefix. The original relative URLs: ./hero-wide.webp, ./square-200.webp, ./square-200.webp, ./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg, ./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp. Need to prefix with ../ (since locale folder). So change to ../hero-wide.webp etc.

Also need to translate frontmatter values: title, subTitle, maybe cover_alt and cover_credit not present. language not present; but we need to set language to "Spanish"? The instruction: Translate only reader-facing frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, and cover_credit. If a language field exists, set it exactly to "Spanish". There's no language field, so we don't add it. Only translate title and subTitle. Keep other keys unchanged.

So frontmatter becomes:

title: "Es hora de las cadenas de conexión llm://"
subTitle: "Simplifica la configuración de modelo y proveedor con URLs `llm://`"

We need to keep same quoting style.

Now translate body.

We need to preserve blockquote class="inset". Inside translate text.

Also preserve code blocks unchanged.

Translate headings: "## The Env Var Explosion" => "## La explosión de variables de entorno". "## Need auth? Great, add it." => "## ¿Necesitas autenticación? Bien, añádela." etc. Keep heading levels.

Also translate other headings: "### Anatomy of an LLM Connection String" => "### Anatomía de una cadena de conexión LLM". "## Resiliency? Why the hell not." => "## ¿Resiliencia? ¿Por qué no?" etc. "## Alternative Formats" => "## Formatos alternativos". "## The Verdict" => "## El veredicto". Also the blockquote with class "ai-response inset". Translate.

Also translate the inline text.

Need to adjust relative URLs in image markdown: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg) => ![las partes de una cadena de conexión LLM](../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg). Also the other image.

Also the blockquote with class "inset" at top: **Update:** This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-levy-llm-uri-scheme/). Translate "Update:" to "Actualización:".

Also the final footnote comment: {/* ¹ Yes, I know that `URI` is more correct than `URL`. If you are pedantic enough to actually care about that distinction, please go touch grass. */} Keep as is? It's a comment, not displayed. Should we translate? It's code comment, but it's not displayed. Probably keep as is, but could translate. Since it's inside comment braces, it's part of source, but it's not displayed. The instruction: translate only reader-facing prose. This comment is not displayed, but it's part of source code. Might keep original. Safer to leave unchanged.

Now translate the prose.

Let's produce final output.
